# GitHub Copilot Instructions

These instructions apply to all GitHub Copilot interactions within this repository and across all sessions.

## Communication Style

- Do not use emojis in any response.
- Use plain, professional language.
- When listing items, use standard markdown bullet points or numbered lists rather than emoji-based symbols.

## Context

This repository belongs to Professor Ruggiero Lovreglio (ProfRino), a researcher in digital construction and fire engineering at Massey University, New Zealand. He also maintains the **Notica-Revit-MCP-Skill** repository.

## Repositories

- [Blender-MCP-Assembly-Skill](https://github.com/ProfRino/Blender-MCP-Assemply-Skill) - A geometry-building skill for creating reliable 3D models in Blender via MCP.
- [Notica-Revit-MCP-Skill](https://github.com/ProfRino/Notica-Revit-MCP-Skill) - Skill files for the Notica Revit MCP, enabling AI assistants to query and control Revit models via natural language.
